task_objective: Harden cold-start launch gating, renewable pause ownership, Syncthing watch handoff, bounded diagnostics, and reusable Decky log regression analysis.
tests_added_or_extended: Pull-script argv, validation, and temp-destination coverage
Real-syntax log parsing, incident correlation, strict exit, malformed/unreadable input, deduplication, and false-positive coverage
Startup tracking hydration and tracking-readiness store coverage
Cold/failed tracking launch gating and awaited Syncthing stop-order coverage
Five-second non-overlapping lease renewal, one shared resume, lease loss, long conflict waits, and controller disposal coverage
Per-PID backend STOP/CONT serialization, exact-generation renewal/resume, retry, expiry revalidation, and RPC compatibility coverage
Cross-phase backend Syncthing watch replacement coverage
Typed structural lifecycle summaries and bounded BrowserView diagnostics coverage

red_evidence.failure: Round-03 review observed 3401 input lines but zero parsed levels and missed all three historical early resumes plus the tracking mismatch; sentinel fixtures contained rule IDs instead of production syntax.

red_evidence.command: ./run.sh uv run pytest tests/test_watchdog.py tests/test_service.py tests/test_main.py tests/test_main_rpc.py tests/test_compatibility.py
red_evidence.failure: Review ordering analysis showed an old SIGCONT could occur after a concurrent new SIGSTOP, while the stale-resume test asserted only dictionary contents; expiry selection could also race a late renewal.

Round-03 review found controller lease-loss coverage absent, mutation Promises created before protection checks, disposal unable to cancel in-flight handlers, and loss/release using different resume settlement paths.
Review found untyped shallow summary tests, missing actual result.overall aggregates, path-bearing free-form fields, malformed Markdown fences, and inaccurate split session records.

The first final round-03 gate reported gameLifecycleController.tsx at 475 lines against the temporarily raised 460-line budget; local contracts were consolidated and the original 450-line budget restored.

design_decisions: Tracking hydration and settings hydration settle independently before startup readiness; tracking failures enter an explicit failed state and cold/failed launches gate conservatively.
Each paused PID owns an opaque renewable lease; frontend releases include the exact lease ID and backend STOP/CONT plus lease generation changes serialize under a per-PID transition lock.
Loss, explicit release, and disposal share one idempotent resume Promise, while protected RPC thunks and lifecycle epochs prevent post-loss or post-disposal mutations.
The watchdog revalidates the exact expired generation after acquiring the transition lock, so a late renewal or replacement lease cannot be resumed from stale inspection.
Log analysis derives stable rule IDs from production syntax and resets bounded correlation state per file; raw Deck logs remain only under /tmp/sdh_ludusavi.
Routine lifecycle diagnostics whitelist typed scalar fields, sanitize all included free-form values, and omit nested save/registry/path payloads.
validation_commands: ./run.sh uv run pytest tests/test_watchdog.py tests/test_service.py tests/test_main.py tests/test_main_rpc.py tests/test_compatibility.py tests/test_watcher.py

./run.sh pnpm run test:unit -- src/runtime/startupHydration.test.ts src/state/ludusaviState.test.tsx src/controllers/launchGateLease.test.ts src/controllers/gameLifecycleController.test.ts src/controllers/gameLifecycleController.logging.test.ts src/controllers/syncthingMonitor.handoffCleanup.test.ts src/controllers/syncthingMonitor.initialization.test.ts src/surfaces/autoSyncStatusBrowserView.test.ts

results.python: 668 tests passed with 86.80% coverage; Ruff check/format and ty passed.
results.hardware_verification: Pending review, finalization onto dev, development prerelease installation, and the deferred Steam Deck scenario in the plan.
